sn4.18:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Magadhans near the brahman village of Pañcasālā.
sn4.18:1.2: And on that occasion there were young people’s presents (being given) in Pañcasālā.
sn4.18:1.3: Then early in the morning, the Blessed One, having adjusted his under robe and carrying his bowl & outer robe, went into Pañcasālā for alms.
sn4.18:1.4: And at that time Māra, the Evil One, had taken possession of the brahman householders of Pañcasālā [with the thought,]
sn4.18:1.5: “Don’t let Gotama the contemplative get alms.”
sn4.18:2.1: Then the Blessed One left Pañcasālā with his bowl as clean as it had been when he entered.
sn4.18:2.2: Then Māra the Evil One went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, said to him:
sn4.18:2.4: “So was it you, Evil One, who arranged that I didn’t get alms?”
sn4.18:2.5: “In that case, lord, let the Blessed One go into Pañcasālā a second time.
sn4.18:2.6: I’ll arrange that you get alms.”
sn4.18:3.1: The Buddha: “You’ve produced demerit, Evil One,
sn4.18:5.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “The Blessed One knows me; the One Well-gone knows me”—vanished right there.
